sections.title: In-flight familiarisation syllabus
sections.body:
ote 1 - PIC must operate to and from La Mole as PF within two months of initial qualification.
Note 2 - With TRI approved by DSAC/SE
Note 3 - To remain qualified, PIC must operate to and from LFTZ within 12 months of last visit.

In flight training - Pilot Qualifications
PIC Qualification is valid for 12 months, provided PIC has validated the qualification within first 2 months. Refer to
Training Syllabus.

Each time a pilot operates to and from LFTZ, the qualification is valid for 12 months from the date of operation.

LA MOLE, FRANCE
LA MOLE
10-10-2P2

-

sections.title: Limitations / Miscellaneous
sections.body:
AY ONLY OPERATIONS
PF shall hold a current AHQ.
Phenom 300 ONLY and CE-680A Latitude
Landing on RWY 24 only and take-off from RWY 06 only; daylight only; PF for approach, landing, and take-off shall be
the PIC occupying the LHS; normal CAT AIR-OPS performance calculations must be used for all operations.

Take-off and landing are prohibited if:

sections.title: Overview
sections.body:
a Mole is a Category C airport located near Saint Tropez. NetJets has special authorisation from DSAC/SE to operate
at LFTZ using the special procedures within this OM-C

La Mole Airport Overview

lines: a Mole is a Category C airport located near Saint Tropez. NetJets has special authorisation from DSAC/SE to operate
at LFTZ using the special procedures within this OM-C

sections.title: Operational procedures — General
sections.body:
a Mole requires extensive visual manoeuvring for the arrival and departure.
Ensure the weather minima is met for the entire visual portion required.
The procedures outlined in this document are designed to minimise noise disturbance and also to avoid infringing
any adjacent airspace.
To assist with Situational Awareness, the map display shall be adjusted to a suitable range scale with all adjacent
and restricted airspace and TAWS overlay (Latitude) displayed on the MFD.
Note Prohibited, Restricted and Danger Areas along with Airspace carefully from the EFB.

LA MOLE, FRANCE
LA MOLE
10-10-2P4

-

sections.title: Arrival procedures
sections.body:
.1 GENERAL
Arrivals to RWY 24 ONLY.
Expect a visual approach RWY 24 via EM (Warehouse SE of Port Grimaud beach).
When the weather is close to the required minima, the VOR A procedure may be used solely as a cloud-breaking
procedure to become visual at or above 2500ft; if VMC can be maintained - carry out an immediate right turn at an
altitude up to 3400ft; maintain VMC below the cloud. Cancel IFR and proceed VFR towards STP. After STP fly east
towards the coast and visually position to EM.
If VMC cannot be obtained, follow the published MAP for the VOR A approach and coordinate with ATC a diversion
to the preferred alternate airport.

9.2  VISUAL APPROACH RWY 24 VIA EM (PORT GRIMAUD)
Procedure:
When coming from the East via Golfe de Saint-Tropez, keep to the centre of the bay in order to minimise
noise on either side of the bay.
Report visual over EM at altitude 2500ft and fully configured.
Descend upon leaving EM and follow track 234° while remaining SE of highway D98 until abeam Cogolin;
Distance from EM to threshold RWY 24 is approx. 5.8NM.
Distance from STP to threshold RWY 24 is 5NM.
Descent rate from EM is approximately 900fpm.
Ensure stable approach criteria are met at 500ft AAL.
When meeting stable approach criteria (wings level by 300ft), continue the approach until the windsock can
clearly be seen and based on the visual assessment, continue the approach, or go around.

Considerations:
When establishing initial contact with the AFIS controller, ask if any other aircraft have reported windshear;
In case of an engine failure before abeam Cogolin, turn left towards STP and climb to an altitude of 2500ft
remaining VMC.
In case of an engine failure after abeam Cogolin, initiate a go-around whilst following the prescribed ground
track for the approach and missed approach.
In the event on an engine failure below 300ft continue to land or initiate a go-around if the engine failure
destabilises the approach.
The windsock is left abeam threshold RWY 24 and offers the best indication of the actual touchdown wind
Reported wind ‘calm’ is used for winds up to 3kt from any direction.
Select Hyeres (LFTH) approach frequency on the spare VHF radio (Com 2).
In case of windshear below 300ft AAL, take into account the low energy state of the aircraft and a potential
continued sink towards the runway during the windshear escape manoeuvre while maintaining the aircraft in
the required pitch attitude.

LA MOLE, FRANCE
LA MOLE
10-10-2P5

-
Approach Configuration:
Flap Full
Landing Gear down
Approach speed Vref+5kt

VISUAL APPROACH RWY 24 VIA EM (NORTH UP)

LA MOLE, FRANCE
LA MOLE
10-10-2P6

-

VISUAL APPROACH RWY 24 VIA EM TRACK UP
9.3  VMC GO-AROUND / BALKED LANDING:
Remain VMC during the go-around procedure.
Fly overhead the runway on runway track until abeam the apron.
Turn right abeam the apron, following the centre of La Mole valley (track 290°).
Abeam ‘station d'epuration’ (water treatment plant) on right hand side, turn left to avoid La Mole village and
continue along the valley, following road D98 (average track 250°).
Clean up altitude 1100ft AEO or 1600ft OEI.
Thereafter climb to 3400ft, weather permitting, or a minimum of 2500ft
At 10 DME STP along the valley, turn left and continue the turn to intercept R250 inbound STP.
Avoid the built-up areas at approximately 5 DME STP and proceed visually to EM for another approach or proceed
to alternate airport.
Report intentions to the AFIS controller as soon as possible.

LA MOLE, FRANCE
LA MOLE
10-10-2P7

-
Performance: In accordance with PDCS data.

VMC GO AROUND / BALKED LANDING

LA MOLE, FRANCE
LA MOLE
10-10-2P8

-

sections.title: Departure procedures
sections.body:
0.1 GENERAL
Departures RWY 06 only. Expect VFR departure as detailed below.
Static take-off only, using full runway length.

Do not fly IFR SIDs as shown on Jeppesen as the required climb gradients cannot be met OEI.
(with a MTOM of 22,000 lb the Latitude is able to reach minimum 12% climb gradient (max 30 deg C OAT and flaps
1)
Take-off is not permitted in the following cases:

sections.title: Qualification requirements
sections.body:
. Wet or contaminated runway
Performance: In accordance with PDCS data.

10.2  VFR DEPARTURE
Procedure:
Initially Rwy HDG 061°
Abeam factory on right hand side (see La Mole airport overview picture), turn LEFT onto approximate HDG
036°
Maintain VMC in the centre of the valley towards the coastline
(direction EM) and when passing 1500'
turn right, maintaining VMC to STP VOR for IFR join in (see VFR departure RWY 06 picture)
Standard flap retraction as per aircraft OM-B.
Then climb at 150kts to max 3400ft maintaining VMC until cleared to join controlled airspace.
Remain clear of controlled airspace until instructed.

Take-off configuration:
Static take-off / using full runway length.
Flaps: As per PDCS data.
Maximum take-off weights in accordance with PDCS data.

LA MOLE, FRANCE
LA MOLE
10-10-2P9

-

VFR DEPARTURE RWY 06

VISUAL APPROACH RWY 24 / VFR DEPARTURE RWY 06
10.3  ENGINE FAILURE PROCEDURE RWY 06
Shortly after departure from RWY 06 - abeam the factory (see La Mole airport overview picture in section 7) - turn
left to visually follow the valley (See Picture - Engine Failure Procedure RWY 06 Track). For reference only, see
tracks dependent on V2 shown in a table in the picture section.
At 3.3 DME STP, but not below 1000 feet, turn right to STP.
Minimum Acceleration Altitude is 1559 ft (as per PDCS data).
Climb to maximum altitude 3400 ft maintaining VMC below cloud.

LA MOLE, FRANCE
LA MOLE
10-10-2P10

sections.title: Miscellaneous
sections.body:
ith Westerly winds in excess of 10Kts caution possible rotor and associated windshear when landing RWY 24.
It is common to have wind from 240 degrees at the threshold 24 and from 140 degrees at mid-point. This is the main
reason for the wind associated restrictions.
Westerly winds are prevailing either as a result of Mistral (all year long but mostly winter months) and sea -breeze
phenomenon in the summer.
Operation is most likely to be disrupted between 1200 and 1700 local time due to breeze in excess of 15 kt.
Caution busy VFR traffic from light aircraft and helicopters particularly in area of EM and STP VOR.
ATIS available by phone: +33 4 94 54 76 62.
